comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Verified in current checkout. extensions/tau/src/app.ts:252-254 builds the agent tool description from a stub registry ({ list: () => [] }) and no longer calls AgentRegistry.load(...) or validateResolvedAgentConfiguration(...) during extension startup. Actual registry loading + validation now occurs in extensions/tau/src/agents-menu/index.ts via loadRegistry(cwd) on session_start/session_switch and command paths. This means startup no longer validates agent definitions globally and the initial agent tool description is empty until a session event refreshes it. Review claim is directionally valid; precise symptom is an empty 'Available agents' section rather than literal 'available agents: none'.

comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Fixed in extensions/tau/src/app.ts: startup now loads AgentRegistry from process.cwd(), runs validateResolvedAgentConfiguration, and builds initial agent tool description from the resolved registry instead of a stub empty registry. Added regression coverage in extensions/tau/test/app-runtime.test.ts: startup now rejects when HOME-level agent settings define an invalid tool for deep (imaginary_tool), proving validation runs during extension startup.
